examples: As the sun rose, we took down our tents and started out across the desert.

phrasalVerbs.definition: start doing sth in a determined or enthusiastic way
examples: We only had a few hours to get the party ready so we set to work quickly.
You’re such a good cook that I think you should set up a restaurant.

phrasalVerbs.definition: become calm after being upset, etc
examples: When his mum left him at school on the first day, Charlie was quite upset, but he soon settled down and started to enjoy himself.

phrasalVerbs.definition: stay in one place or get married and live quietly
examples: She spent her twenties travelling round the world and then settled down in a quiet village in Sussex.

phrasalVerbs.definition: take sb on a tour of a place
examples: Let me show you round the garden.

phrasalVerbs.definition: try to attract people’s attention and make them admire you (usually used negatively)
examples: My sister thinks she’s a good singer and she’s always showing off when people come to visit.
The train started to slow down as it approached the station.
I realised that I might not have enough time to finish the exam so I started to speed up.
